seoTitle: Edith vs Edmondson Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Edith and Edmondson Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Edith vs Edmondson Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Edith and Edmondson Park.
heading: Edith vs Edmondson Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Edith, New South Wales and Edmondson Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Edmondson Park has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Edith (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Edith or Edmondson Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Edith has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Edmondson Park scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Edith scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Edmondson Park scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Edith or Edmondson Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Edmondson Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
